quoteFirst: Some _sweeps_, residing in Charles Street, having been married, they resolved to celebrate the day, and,
quoteTime: about eight o'clock in the evening
quoteLast: , the bride and bridegroom, attended by eleven couples more, all mounted on asses, and followed by several hundreds of spectators, with tin pots, horns, dust bells, watchmen's rattles, flambeaux, etc., proceeded through Drury Lane, and made their grand entrance into Holborn up Newton Street to the Bank public-house, where they stopped to get some refreshment; but in forming the procession again, the bride's Arabian was unfortunately thrown down by the pressure of the mob, and the lady precipitated in the mud.

quoteFirst: In this hurricane almost all our sails were split, and great part of our standing rigging broken; and,
